name: As Neves - return via San Fins Viewpoint
name_original: As Neves - vuelta por Mirador de San Fins
region_intro: Mountain biking around As Neves, located in southern Galicia, Spain, features a diverse natural landscape shaped by river valleys and mountains. The region is characterized by the Miño River, which forms a natural border, and the valleys of the Xuliana and Termes rivers. To the north, the Paradanta Mountains provide varied terrain, including both riverside paths and more challenging mountainous areas. This combination of water features and elevated terrain creates a dynamic environment for mountain bike routes. Best mountain bike trails around As Neves The most popular mountain bike trail is As Neves - return via San Fins Viewpoint, a difficult 26.5 miles (42.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain and panoramic views from San Fins Viewpoint. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Minho River Greenway – Viewpoint of the Miño River loop from Sela, a moderate 35.8 miles (57.7 km) path. This route follows the Miño River, providing scenic riverside riding opportunities. Local mountain bikers also love the Bridge of the Partitions – Fillaboa Medieval Bridge loop from As Neves, a 55.9 miles (90.0 km) trail leading through historical sites and river crossings, often completed in about 8 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around As Neves is defined by the Miño River valley, the Xuliana river's valley, and the Paradanta Mountains. The network of mountain bike routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in As Neves are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore As Neves's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around As Neves?
A: There are over 90 mountain bike trails around As Neves, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The region's diverse landscape, shaped by river valleys and mountains, provides a dynamic environment for exploration.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in As Neves?
A: As Neves offers a good mix of trails for various abilities. You'll find 3 easy routes, 53 moderate routes, and 37 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both beginners and experienced mountain bikers can find suitable challenges.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in As Neves?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the region's numerous nature and walking trails, particularly those along the Miño River, often provide gentler terrain suitable for families. Look for routes described as 'easy' or 'moderate' for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in As Neves?
A: The terrain in As Neves is quite varied. You'll encounter riverside paths along the Miño and Xuliana rivers, as well as more challenging mountainous areas in the Paradanta Mountains. This combination means routes can range from relatively flat and smooth to steep and technical, often featuring historical sites and natural viewpoints.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails in As Neves pass by significant landmarks and natural features. You might encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2886707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Coutada Petroglyphs&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Troña&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2499686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto San Nomedio Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic Miño River valley.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around As Neves are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6951517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Nomedio Geodesic Point – Altamira Hillforts loop from As Neves&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive views and historical interest.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in As Neves?
A: The mountain bike trails in As Neves are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 90 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views of the Miño River and surrounding valleys, and the opportunity to explore historical sites along the routes.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain biking options in As Neves?
A: For those seeking longer rides, As Neves offers several extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Partitions – Fillaboa Medieval Bridge loop from As Neves&lt;/a&gt; is a significant 55.9-mile (90.0 km) trail that takes approximately 8 hours and 40 minutes to complete, leading through historical sites and river crossings.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in As Neves?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the diverse natural landscape of As Neves, with its rivers and mountains, generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking. These seasons typically offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, avoiding the peak heat of summer or potential winter rainfall.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Miño River?
A: Yes, the Miño River forms a significant part of the region's landscape, and several trails follow its course. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minho River Greenway – Viewpoint of the Miño River loop from Sela&lt;/a&gt;, which provides picturesque riverside riding opportunities and scenic views.

Q: Where can I find viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many routes in As Neves offer rewarding viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975661650/as-neves-return-via-san-fins-viewpoint" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Neves - return via San Fins Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; trail, for instance, is known for its significant elevation gain and panoramic views from San Fins Viewpoint. The Mirador del Miño is another notable spot for expansive vistas of the river.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the Xuliana River valley?
A: Yes, the Xuliana River valley is another prominent feature of the area. Routes like the Ruta Muíños do Río Xuliana (Mills of the Xuliana River Route) highlight the historical use of hydraulic energy with its mills and lead to the river's cascade into the Miño, offering interesting features for exploration.
tips: The top of Alto San Nomedio (Coto Grande) at 690m altitude. At the same top we will find the hermitage of San Nomedio and spectacular views
The Alto de San Fins is almost 700m above sea level. In its upper part we can find the Hermitage of San Fins. From the top you can enjoy magnificent views of the Miño river valley

waypoints.name: Alto San Nomedio Summit and Hermitage
